summary.summary: Patch release (Trio v0.8.1) that rolls three tracks: (1) adds loopandlearn/OmnipodKit as a new submodule and surfaces it in the Add Pump flow as “All Omnipod Types” (Eros + DASH unified); pod type (Eros vs. DASH) is chosen at pod activation and the new driver is opt‑in while OmniKit and OmniBLE remain unchanged (existing users of those drivers are not changed) (pr:gordolio/Trio#1174, pr:nightscout/Trio#1174). (2) Security/tooling: bumps fastlane 2.233.1 → 2.235.0 which allows jwt 3.2.0+ to be resolved and addresses a Dependabot alert (pr:gordolio/Trio#1174, pr:nightscout/Trio#1174). (3) Fix: telemetry daily sends were not being throttled to once per day and the daily-send logic is now enforced (pr:gordolio/Trio#1174, pr:nightscout/Trio#1174). The change is recorded in merges into the patch branch (commit:176e61d335060d434c2d4089882d3987f28c03f3, commit:194c9b9e720cdb1987b1a8e687d0264b1162efdf).
summary.userFacingImpact: Conservative summary of likely user impact based on the supplied sources: (1) The OmnipodKit driver is a new, opt‑in pump manager surfaced as “All Omnipod Types”; users must explicitly select it (pod type chosen at activation) — existing users of OmniKit and OmniBLE should see no behavior change unless they choose the new driver (pr:gordolio/Trio#1174, pr:nightscout/Trio#1174). (2) The fastlane/jwt bump is described as CI/release tooling only and not a shipped-app code surface change in the sources (pr:gordolio/Trio#1174, pr:nightscout/Trio#1174). (3) Telemetry background pings should now be correctly throttled to once per day (pr:gordolio/Trio#1174, pr:nightscout/Trio#1174). If you need verification of runtime behavior (for example, end-to-end validation that existing pump workflows are unaffected or that telemetry rate changes occur on devices), human review and testing are recommended (commit:176e61d335060d434c2d4089882d3987f28c03f3, commit:194c9b9e720cdb1987b1a8e687d0264b1162efdf).
